description: Improvement Paths & Rebate Persistence - Wire Improve tab to use real ImprovementPath component with context data, create Rebate and ImprovementPath contexts, generate personalized paths from symptoms, replace all mock data

userStories.title: Create Rebate and ImprovementPath models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need data models for rebates and improvement paths so data has a consistent shape across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/models/Rebate.ts with Rebate interface: id (string), equipmentType (RebateEquipmentType), savingsAmount (number), programName (string), programDescription (string), eligibility (EligibilityStatus), eligibilityRequirements (string[]), howToClaim (ClaimStep[]), expirationDate (string | null), additionalInfo (string | null)
Create ClaimStep interface in Rebate.ts: step (number), title (string), description (string), requiresProfessional (boolean)

Create src/models/ImprovementPathData.ts with ImprovementPathData interface: id (string), title (string), description (string), sourceSymptomIds (string[]), steps (ImprovementStep[] from components), createdAt (string, ISO)
Export all new types from src/models/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: RebateEquipmentType and EligibilityStatus already exist in components/RebateCard. ImprovementStep already exists in components/ImprovementPath. Import these types, don't duplicate.

userStories.title: Create RebateContext with AsyncStorage persistence and seed data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a RebateContext to persist rebates and provide them across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/context/RebateContext.tsx following EquipmentContext pattern
AsyncStorage key: @pearl_rebates
State: rebates (Rebate[]), isLoaded (boolean)
Methods: getRebateById(id: string) returns Rebate | undefined, addRebate(rebate: Rebate), updateRebate(id: string, updates: Partial<Rebate>)

On first load when storage is empty, seed with DEFAULT_REBATES array of 5 rebates: Federal Heat Pump Tax Credit (heatPump, $2000, eligible), Home Energy Rebate Program (insulation, $1600, likely), Heat Pump Water Heater Rebate (waterHeater, $1750, eligible), Energy Efficient Windows Credit (windows, $600, check), Residential Clean Energy Credit (solar, $7500, eligible)
Each seeded rebate must include full programDescription, eligibilityRequirements array, and howToClaim steps — combine data from ImproveScreen MOCK_REBATES and RebateDetailScreen REBATE_DATA patterns
Provider gates on isLoaded (returns null until loaded)
Export RebateProvider and useRebates from src/context/index.ts
Add RebateProvider to App.tsx provider chain
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Seed data is critical — Improve tab must show rebates on first launch. Use the EquipmentContext pattern exactly. RebateEquipmentType and EligibilityStatus come from components/RebateCard.

userStories.title: Create ImprovementPathContext with AsyncStorage persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an ImprovementPathContext to persist generated improvement paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/context/ImprovementPathContext.tsx following EquipmentContext pattern
AsyncStorage key: @pearl_improvement_paths
State: paths (ImprovementPathData[]), isLoaded (boolean)

Methods: addPath(path: ImprovementPathData), deletePath(id: string), getPathById(id: string) returns ImprovementPathData | undefined, getPathsForSymptom(symptomId: string) returns paths where sourceSymptomIds includes the symptomId
Provider gates on isLoaded (returns null until loaded)
Export ImprovementPathProvider and useImprovementPaths from src/context/index.ts
Add ImprovementPathProvider to App.tsx provider chain
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ImprovementPathData uses ImprovementStep from components/ImprovementPath. This context stores generated paths that persist across restarts.

userStories.title: Create ImprovementPathService to generate paths from symptoms
userStories.description: As a user, I want Pearl to generate personalized improvement paths based on my logged symptoms.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/services/ImprovementPathService.ts
Export generatePathForSymptom(symptomId: string, symptomLabel: string, categoryId: string): ImprovementPathData
Function is synchronous (pure function, no async needed)
Maps all 17 symptom IDs from SymptomLogModal to contextual improvement paths with 3-5 steps
Each path has steps across multiple phases: at minimum now + soon, most include later, some include major
Now phase steps: free or cheap ($0, $0-30) quick checks the user can do themselves — impact low/medium
Soon phase steps: moderate cost (~$100-300) professional inspections or assessments — impact medium, hasPearlPro true
Later phase steps: significant cost ($500-3000) targeted fixes — impact high, some have hasRebate true
Major phase steps: large cost ($5000-15000) full system replacements — impact high, hasRebate true, hasPearlPro true
Path title format: 'Your [Symptom Label] Plan' (e.g., 'Your Hot Rooms Plan')
Path description format: brief 1-sentence context (e.g., 'Steps to address hot rooms, from quick checks to long-term fixes')
Each step has unique id: symptomId-phase-index (e.g., 'hot-rooms-now-1')
Export from src/services/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core intelligence of the feature. Each of the 17 symptoms gets a unique path. Steps should feel practical and real. Example for hot-rooms: Now: Check vents open, change filter ($0-30, low) / Soon: Get duct inspection (~$150, medium, hasPearlPro) / Later: Seal duct leaks or add insulation ($500-2000, high, hasRebate) / Major: HVAC upgrade ($8000-15000, high, hasRebate, hasPearlPro).

userStories.title: Wire ImproveScreen to use ImprovementPath component with context data
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see personalized improvement paths on the Improve tab instead of placeholder cards.
acceptanceCriteria: ImproveScreen imports useImprovementPaths from context
ImproveScreen imports useSymptoms from context
ImproveScreen imports useRebates from context
ImproveScreen imports ImprovementPath component and ImprovementStep type from components
ImproveScreen imports generatePathForSymptom from services
Remove MOCK_IMPROVEMENT_PATHS constant and local ImprovementPath interface
Remove MOCK_REBATES constant and local Rebate interface
Rebates section reads from useRebates().rebates instead of MOCK_REBATES
On mount (useEffect), for each logged symptom that does not yet have a path in context, call generatePathForSymptom and save via addPath
Improvement Paths section renders ImprovementPath component for each path (max 3, sorted by createdAt descending)
Each ImprovementPath gets onStepPress, onRebatePress, onPearlProPress callbacks

If no symptoms logged AND no paths exist, show empty state: icon map-outline, title 'Your Improvement Paths', description 'Log a symptom on the Home tab to get a personalized step-by-step improvement plan.'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the biggest story — wires everything together. Key: the auto-generation useEffect should check if a path already exists for each symptom's symptomId (via getPathsForSymptom) before generating. Use the EmptyState component for the no-symptoms state. Max 3 paths prevents scroll overwhelm.

userStories.title: Wire RebateDetailScreen to read from RebateContext
userStories.description: As a user, I want rebate detail to show real data from context instead of hardcoded mock data.
acceptanceCriteria: RebateDetailScreen imports useRebates from context
Uses getRebateById(rebateId) to get rebate data instead of REBATE_DATA[rebateId] lookup
Remove REBATE_DATA constant, local RebateInfo interface, and DEFAULT_REBATE_ID constant
If rebate not found (getRebateById returns undefined), show a centered fallback message: 'This rebate is no longer available.' with a back button
All existing UI stays the same when rebate is found (hero, eligibility, requirements, how-to-claim, actions)
ClaimStep interface comes from models import instead of local definition
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Straightforward context wiring. The Rebate model has the same fields as the local RebateInfo interface. Remove all local type definitions and REBATE_DATA mock. Equipment icon and label mappings stay local (they're display-only).

userStories.title: Wire ImproveScreen wrapper to pass navigation callbacks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need ImproveScreen's improvement path and rebate actions to navigate correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: ImproveScreenWrapper in BottomTabNavigator passes onImprovementPathPress callback that console.logs the path ID (future detail screen)
ImproveScreen accepts onStepRebatePress prop — called when user taps View Rebate on a step, passes rebateId, navigates to RebateDetail
ImproveScreen accepts onStepPearlProPress prop — called when user taps Find Pro on a step, navigates to PearlPros
ImproveScreenWrapper passes onStepRebatePress that navigates to RebateDetail
ImproveScreenWrapper passes onStepPearlProPress that navigates to PearlPros
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Small wiring in BottomTabNavigator.tsx. onStepRebatePress navigates with a default rebateId matching the step context. onStepPearlProPress navigates to PearlPros.
